description: Exhaustive refinement of the go-prompt module: fix terminal rendering issues, implement multiline input support, achieve cross-platform correctness, and comprehensive test coverage.

tasks.title: Fix line-wrap rendering bug causing spurious newlines on macOS Terminal
tasks.description: When input + prefix width exceeds terminal columns, the renderer miscalculates cursor positions during clear/redraw cycles. positionAtEndOfStringLine does not handle the exact-fill case (right == columns) that positionAtEndOfString does, and countInputLines has a similar gap. This causes the clear function to move the cursor incorrectly, leaving artifacts (extra newlines above the prompt). Fix all three functions to be consistent and correct for wrapped lines. The clear method itself should also use absolute positioning (\r + column) instead of relative cursor moves that are fragile when the terminal has reflowed.
tasks.acceptance: No spurious newlines appear when input text wraps across terminal width on macOS Terminal. positionAtEndOfStringLine handles right==columns. countInputLines correctly counts soft-wrapped lines. Existing position_test.go, renderer_test.go all pass. New test cases for exact-fill wrapping added.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for line-wrap edge cases in position and renderer
tasks.description: Add test cases to position_test.go for: text that exactly fills columns (right==columns produces wrap), text one char short of columns, text one char over columns, text with wide characters (CJK) that don't fit at line boundary, multiline text where a line wraps mid-grapheme. Add renderer countInputLines tests for the same scenarios.
tasks.acceptance: New tests cover all edge cases listed. Tests pass on Darwin. Tests use only stdlib testing package.

tasks.title: Fix renderer clear method to use robust cursor positioning
tasks.description: The current clear method uses SaveCursor/UnSaveCursor (ESC [s / ESC [u) which are DECSC/DECRC in some terminals but SCO in others, and not reliably supported in macOS Terminal.app. Replace with absolute positioning using CursorUp + \r (carriage return to column 0). The move function uses CursorBackward which doesn't work across line boundaries. Refactor move/clear to use CursorUp(n) + \r for vertical/horizontal repositioning, which is universally supported.
tasks.acceptance: Renderer clear method no longer uses SaveCursor/UnSaveCursor. Uses CursorUp + \r for reliable repositioning. All existing tests pass. Manual verification on macOS Terminal.app shows no rendering artifacts.

tasks.title: Fix completion dropdown position calculation for wrapped input lines
tasks.description: In renderCompletion, the cursor position is calculated for placing the dropdown relative to the cursor. When input wraps, the X position used for the dropdown may exceed the terminal width, causing the backward() call to produce incorrect positioning. Ensure the completion column is clamped and accounts for soft-wrapped lines.
tasks.acceptance: Completion dropdown renders correctly when input text wraps. No visual corruption. Tested with harness tests showing completions with long input.

tasks.title: Add termtest harness integration test for line-wrap rendering
tasks.description: Write an integration test using the termtest.Harness that: (1) creates a prompt with a narrow terminal width (e.g. 20 cols), (2) types text that exceeds the width causing a wrap, (3) triggers completion, (4) verifies the output does not contain spurious newlines or corrupt rendering. Use WriteSync for deterministic synchronization.
tasks.acceptance: Test passes reliably on Darwin. Uses termtest.Harness with narrow columns. Verifies output via Console.Expect with appropriate Conditions.

tasks.title: Fix countInputLines to handle lines that exactly fill terminal width
tasks.description: countInputLines iterates characters and increments lineNumber when lineCharIndex >= col. But when a line exactly fills the column width (lineCharIndex == col), it starts a new line. If the next character is a newline, that newline should not count as an additional line. Audit and fix the interaction between soft-wrap counting and explicit newlines.
tasks.acceptance: countInputLines returns correct count for: text exactly filling one line, text exactly filling multiple lines, text with trailing newline at exact fill point. Unit tests added.

tasks.title: Handle exact column fill in positionAtEndOfStringLine
tasks.description: positionAtEndOfString correctly wraps when right == columns (sets right=0, down++). positionAtEndOfStringLine does NOT have this case. Add the right == columns handling to positionAtEndOfStringLine to be consistent. This is needed for correct cursor positioning when rendering wrapped text with startLine offset.
tasks.acceptance: positionAtEndOfStringLine handles right == columns same as positionAtEndOfString. Added test case 'exact column fill wraps' to position_test.go.

tasks.title: Ensure Ctrl+J inserts literal newline when executeOnEnterCallback says no
tasks.description: In feed(), Enter/ControlJ/ControlM all call executeOnEnterCallback. Currently Ctrl+J (ControlJ) is treated identically to Enter. For multiline support, the design should allow the executeOnEnterCallback to differentiate between Enter (which might submit) and Ctrl+J (which should always insert a newline). However, changing the callback signature is a breaking change. Instead, expose the triggering key via the Prompt API so callbacks can check it. Document that Ctrl+J can be used for literal newline insertion by configuring executeOnEnterCallback appropriately.
tasks.acceptance: Ctrl+J inserts a literal newline when executeOnEnterCallback returns execute=false. Prompt exposes the triggering key to the callback (or uses Document.LastKeyStroke). No breaking API changes. Tests verify Ctrl+J inserts newline while Enter submits.

tasks.title: Add API to distinguish Enter from Ctrl+J in ExecuteOnEnterCallback
tasks.description: The ExecuteOnEnterCallback currently receives (prompt, indentSize) but has no way to know which key triggered it (Enter vs Ctrl+J). The buffer already stores lastKeyStroke. Ensure that Document.LastKeyStroke() is accessible within the callback via p.Buffer().Document().LastKeyStroke(). Add a helper method on Prompt like LastKeyStroke() Key that delegates. Document this pattern for multiline usage.
tasks.acceptance: Prompt.LastKeyStroke() returns the key that triggered the current callback. ExecuteOnEnterCallback implementations can distinguish Enter (submit) from ControlJ (insert newline). Example added to documentation or _example directory.

tasks.title: Design and implement freeform text input mode
tasks.description: Implement a mode where Enter inserts a newline (like a text editor) and a specific key combination (e.g., Ctrl+D on empty line, or configurable submit key) submits the text. This is the 'agentic TUI' pattern where the default is freeform multiline editing. The mode should be configurable via a new Option, e.g. WithFreeformMode(submitKey Key). In freeform mode: Enter inserts newline, the submit key executes. Ctrl+J also inserts newline. The existing executeOnEnterCallback should still be invoked but with the appropriate context. This must NOT break the existing default behavior (Enter submits).
tasks.acceptance: New WithFreeformMode option available. When enabled, Enter inserts newline, configurable submit key submits input. Ctrl+J also inserts newline. Default behavior (Enter submits) unchanged. Integration test via termtest verifies both modes.

tasks.title: Implement dynamic mode switching based on input prefix
tasks.description: Support the Claude Code pattern where input starting with '!' switches to a different mode (e.g., bash mode with different completions and submission semantics). Implement a ModeResolver callback that receives the current buffer text and returns the active mode configuration (completer, executeOnEnterCallback, etc). When the mode changes mid-input, the prompt reconfigures dynamically. This requires a new Option like WithModeResolver(fn func(text string) *ModeConfig) and a ModeConfig struct. Design must allow entirely different completion behavior per mode.
tasks.acceptance: WithModeResolver option available. ModeConfig struct defined with Completer, ExecuteOnEnterCallback, optional Prefix to strip before execution. Mode changes dynamically as user types. Completions update when mode changes. Integration test with two modes (default and !-prefixed) passes.

tasks.title: Add comprehensive tests for cursor navigation in multiline buffers
tasks.description: Ensure cursor up/down in multiline text (with explicit newlines and soft-wrapped lines) moves correctly. Test CursorUp/CursorDown with preferredColumn preservation. Test navigating across a soft-wrap boundary vs a hard newline boundary. Test with wide characters.
tasks.acceptance: New test cases in buffer_test.go or document_test.go for multiline cursor navigation. Cover: up/down across hard newlines, up/down across soft wraps, preferred column preservation, wide character boundaries.

tasks.title: Add termtest integration test for Ctrl+J multiline input
tasks.description: Write a termtest.Harness integration test that: types some text, presses Ctrl+J to insert a newline, types more text, presses Enter to submit. Verify the executed command contains the literal newline. Verify the terminal output shows multiline prefix characters.
tasks.acceptance: Test creates harness, sends text + Ctrl+J + more text + Enter. Asserts executed command contains \n. Asserts terminal output shows multiline prefix dots. Test passes reliably.

tasks.title: Add termtest integration test for freeform text input mode
tasks.description: Write a termtest.Harness integration test that creates a prompt in freeform mode, types text, presses Enter (expecting newline insertion), types more text, then presses the configured submit key. Verify the executed command contains newlines from Enter presses. Verify correct rendering of multiline input.
tasks.acceptance: Test uses WithFreeformMode, sends text + Enter + text + submit key. Asserts executed command has embedded newlines. Test passes reliably.

tasks.title: Add termtest integration test for dynamic mode switching
tasks.description: Write a termtest.Harness integration test that creates a prompt with a ModeResolver (default mode + '!' bash mode). Test: (1) type normal text → completions from default completer, (2) clear and type '!ls' → completions from bash completer, (3) submit → executed command is 'ls' (prefix stripped). Verify mode switching is seamless.
tasks.acceptance: Test creates harness with mode resolver. Verifies completions change based on prefix. Verifies prefix stripping on execution. Passes reliably.

tasks.title: Fix prepareArea ScrollDown/ScrollUp to be compatible with macOS Terminal
tasks.description: prepareArea uses ESC D (Index) and ESC M (Reverse Index). ESC D in some terminals is 'cursor down' not 'scroll'. On macOS Terminal.app, this can cause unexpected behavior. Consider using \n for scrolling down (universally supported) instead of ESC D. Or use the scroll region approach. Verify behavior on macOS Terminal.app.
tasks.acceptance: prepareArea works correctly on macOS Terminal.app. No spurious scrolling or newlines. If ESC D is replaced, replacement is universally compatible.

tasks.title: Audit VT100Writer for terminal compatibility issues
tasks.description: Review all escape sequences in writer_vt100.go for compatibility with: macOS Terminal.app, iTerm2, Linux console, xterm, Windows Terminal. Specifically verify: SaveCursor/UnSaveCursor (ESC [s/u vs ESC 7/8), ScrollDown (ESC D vs \n), CursorGoTo (1-based vs 0-based). Document any incompatibilities found.
tasks.acceptance: All escape sequences reviewed. Any incompatibilities documented in code comments. Fixes applied where needed for macOS Terminal.app. No behavioral changes for working terminals.

tasks.title: Ensure all tests pass on Darwin (macOS)
tasks.description: Run gmake make-all-with-log and verify all prompt module tests pass. Fix any failures. The termtest package has Darwin-specific reader code (reader_darwin.go) that must work correctly.
tasks.acceptance: gmake make-all-with-log exits 0. All prompt tests pass. No test flakiness observed on repeated runs.

tasks.title: Ensure all tests pass on Linux (container)
tasks.description: Run gmake make-all-in-container and verify all prompt module tests pass. Fix any failures. The termtest package has Linux-specific reader code (reader_linux.go) that must work correctly.
tasks.acceptance: gmake make-all-in-container exits 0. All prompt tests pass in the Linux container.

tasks.title: Ensure all tests pass on Windows
tasks.description: Run gmake make-all-run-windows and verify all prompt module tests pass. The prompt module has Windows-specific files (reader_windows.go, writer_windows.go) that must compile and work. Fix any compilation or test failures.
tasks.acceptance: gmake make-all-run-windows exits 0. All prompt tests pass on Windows. writer_windows.go and reader_windows.go compile correctly.

tasks.title: Audit completion hide/show state transitions for consistency
tasks.description: Review the interaction between hideCompletionsAfterExecute, showCompletionsForUserIntent, completionHiddenByExecute, and the completion manager's IsHidden/Hide/Show methods. Ensure there are no edge cases where completions are incorrectly shown or hidden after mode switches, Ctrl+C, or history navigation.
tasks.acceptance: All state transitions documented. Any bugs found are fixed with regression tests. No existing test regressions.

tasks.title: Document multiline input usage patterns in README
tasks.description: Update prompt/README.md to document: (1) how to use executeOnEnterCallback for multiline input, (2) the new Ctrl+J behavior for literal newlines, (3) the freeform mode option, (4) the mode switching capability. Include code examples.
tasks.acceptance: README.md updated with clear documentation of multiline features. Code examples are correct and runnable.

tasks.title: Update CHANGELOG.md with all changes
tasks.description: Add entries to prompt/CHANGELOG.md under [Unreleased] for all fixes and features implemented in this effort: rendering fixes, multiline input support, mode switching, new options, new test coverage.
tasks.acceptance: CHANGELOG.md has entries for all changes following Keep a Changelog format.

tasks.title: Ensure staticcheck passes with zero warnings
tasks.description: Run staticcheck on the prompt module and fix any warnings. The project uses staticcheck as part of the standard build checks.
tasks.acceptance: staticcheck reports zero warnings for the prompt module.

tasks.title: Ensure go vet passes with zero warnings
tasks.description: Run go vet on the prompt module and fix any warnings.
tasks.acceptance: go vet ./... reports zero warnings for the prompt module.

tasks.title: Ensure betteralign passes
tasks.description: Run betteralign on the prompt module and fix any alignment issues in struct definitions.
tasks.acceptance: betteralign reports no issues for the prompt module.
